 {"id":"caam-fhrk","title":"TUI: Add post-mutation state refresh and error handling","description":"After any mutation (activate, delete, refresh), TUI state must be refreshed.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Create refreshProfiles() method that reloads all profiles from vault
2. Call after every successful mutation
3. Preserve selected index if possible (may need to adjust if profile deleted)
4. Create showError() helper for consistent error display

ERROR HANDLING PATTERNS:
- File not found: 'Profile not found in vault'
- Permission denied: 'Cannot write to auth file - check permissions'
- Vault corrupted: 'Profile data corrupted - try re-backup'

SUCCESS MESSAGES:
- Activate: 'Activated {profile} for {provider}'
- Delete: 'Deleted {profile}'
- Refresh: 'Refreshed {profile} - new token valid until {date}'

 {"id":"caam-fiw4","title":"Task: Batch /login injection via WezTerm","description":"Goal
- Provide a single command to inject /login into all matched panes with optional subscription selection.

Details
- Command shape: caam wezterm login-all <tool> with flags --subscription, --all, --dry-run, --yes, --match.
- Use wezterm cli send-text --pane-id with --no-paste to avoid bracketed paste issues.
- Default behavior: dry-run or confirmation prompt unless --yes is provided.
- Print per-pane results and a summary (targeted, skipped, failed).
- Never send to panes that are not matched unless --all is explicit.

Notes
- A local implementation already exists in the working tree; ensure it is validated, documented, and merged with tests.

Success
- Batch login is safe, predictable, and low-friction.","notes":"Depends on: caam-sssb (pane discovery).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:27:31.195144554Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T09:30:50.906323573Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T09:30:50.906323573Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-fiw4","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:27:31.197032460Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-fiw4","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:31:02.868930611Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-fiw4","depends_on_id":"caam-sssb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:55:18.511509399Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-fj9a","title":"Integrate identity extraction into Profile loading","description":"# Task: Integrate identity extraction into Profile loading

## Overview
Automatically extract and attach identity information when loading profiles, so users see rich identity data in commands like `caam ls` and `caam status`.

## Technical Details

### Profile Enhancement
```go
// internal/profile/profile.go

type Profile struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // NEW: Identity information extracted from auth files
    Identity *identity.Identity `json:"identity,omitempty"`
}

// LoadWithIdentity loads profile and extracts identity
func (p *Profile) LoadWithIdentity() error {
    // 1. Load profile as normal
    // 2. Determine provider
    // 3. Call appropriate identity extractor
    // 4. Attach to profile
}
```

### Display Enhancement
```
$ caam ls claude

PROFILES FOR CLAUDE:
  NAME               EMAIL                 PLAN      STATUS
  alice              alice@gmail.com       Max       Active
  bob                bob@company.com       Team      Ready
  work-account       unknown               unknown   Ready
```

### Rotation Enhancement
Enterprise/Team accounts may have different limits - use identity.PlanType in rotation scoring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identity extracted on profile load (lazy, cached)
- [ ] `caam ls` shows email and plan type
- [ ] `caam status` shows full identity details
- [ ] Rotation considers plan type in scoring
- [ ] Graceful fallback when identity unavailable

## Dependencies
- Implement JWT parser for Codex (caam-vek1)
- Implement Claude identity parser (caam-j6rg)
- Implement Gemini identity parser (caam-v021)

## Files to Modify
- internal/profile/profile.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/ls.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/status.go
- internal/rotation/scoring.go

-{"id":"caam-fnkk","title":"TUI: Color-code status messages (red for errors, green for success)","description":"Goal
- Color-code status messages (red for errors, yellow for warnings, green for success) instead of always gray.

Details
- Update status styling logic to map severity to color tokens from the theme system.
- Ensure status colors remain readable in light/dark and NO_COLOR modes.
- Avoid hiding key hints or overwriting status chips.

Tests
- Unit tests for severity -> style mapping.
- Golden snapshot tests for success/warn/error statuses.
- Structured test logs with severity and applied style.

Success
- Status messages visually convey severity at a glance.

 {"id":"caam-fu2w","title":"EPIC: Live Usage Monitor & Alerts","description":"# EPIC: Live Usage Monitor

## Strategic Context

When managing multiple AI accounts, users want real-time visibility into the status of all accounts. This is different from the TUI (which focuses on profile management) - this is a MONITORING dashboard that shows live rate limit status, refresh continuously, and alerts when any account needs attention.

## Use Cases

1. **Background Monitoring**: Keep a terminal window showing all account status
2. **tmux/Screen Integration**: Show status in status bar
3. **Alert Notifications**: Desktop notifications when limits approaching
4. **Scripting Integration**: Machine-readable status for custom workflows

## User Experience

### Interactive Monitor
```
$ caam monitor

╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    LIVE USAGE MONITOR                                  ║
║                    Last updated: 12:34:56                              ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║                                                                         ║
║  CLAUDE                                                                 ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  alice@gmail.com  🟢 ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42% │ resets 4h 12m │ ready  ║
║  bob@gmail.com    🟡 ██████████████░░░░░░ 71% │ resets 1h 45m │ ready  ║
║  carol@gmail.com  🔴 ████████████████████ 95% │ resets 0h 23m │ cooldown║
║                                                                         ║
║  CODEX                                                                  ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  work@company.com 🟢 ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 18% │ resets 3h 56m │ ready  ║
║                                                                         ║
║  GEMINI                                                                 ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  (no profiles configured)                                               ║
║                                                                         ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
  Refresh: 30s │ [r] refresh now │ [p] precheck │ [q] quit
```

### tmux Integration
```bash
# In .tmux.conf
set -g status-right '#(caam monitor --format brief)'

# Shows: claude:42%▲ codex:18%▲
```

### Alert Mode
```
$ caam monitor --alert --threshold 80

[2024-01-10 12:34:56] ⚠️  claude/carol@gmail.com at 85%
[2024-01-10 12:35:12] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 90%
[2024-01-10 12:35:45] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 95% - SWITCH RECOMMENDED
```

## Implementation

### Monitor State
```go
// internal/monitor/monitor.go

type Monitor struct {
    interval   time.Duration
    providers  []string
    
    fetcher    *usage.MultiProfileFetcher
    vault      *authfile.Vault
    healthStore *health.Storage
    db         *db.DB
    
    state      *MonitorState
    mu         sync.RWMutex
}

type MonitorState struct {
    Profiles  map[string]*ProfileState  // "provider/name" -> state
    UpdatedAt time.Time
    Errors    []string
}

type ProfileState struct {
    Provider     string
    ProfileName  string
    Usage        *usage.UsageInfo
    Health       health.HealthStatus
    PoolStatus   authpool.PoolStatus
    InCooldown   bool
    CooldownUntil *time.Time
    Alert        *Alert
}

type Alert struct {
    Type    AlertType
    Message string
    Since   time.Time
}
```

### Output Renderers
```go
// internal/monitor/render.go

type Renderer interface {
    Render(state *MonitorState) string
}

type TableRenderer struct {}    // Rich ASCII table
type BriefRenderer struct {}    // One-line summary
type JSONRenderer struct {}     // Machine-readable
type AlertRenderer struct {}    // Alerts only
```

### Alert System
```go
// internal/monitor/alerts.go

type AlertManager struct {
    threshold   float64
    notifier    Notifier
    history     []*Alert
}

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(alert *Alert) error
}

type DesktopNotifier struct {}  // libnotify / osascript
type TerminalNotifier struct {} // Print to stderr
type WebhookNotifier struct {}  // POST to URL
```

### Background Polling
```go
func (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return nil
        case <-ticker.C:
            m.refresh(ctx)
        }
    }
}

func (m *Monitor) refresh(ctx context.Context) {
    // 1. List all profiles from vault
    // 2. Fetch usage for each (concurrent)
    // 3. Check health status
    // 4. Check cooldown status
    // 5. Generate alerts
    // 6. Update state
}
```

## Commands

### caam monitor
```
Usage:
  caam monitor [flags]

Flags:
  -i, --interval duration   Refresh interval (default 30s)
  -p, --provider strings    Providers to monitor (default: all)
  -f, --format string       Output format: table, brief, json, alerts
  -a, --alert               Enable alert mode (log alerts to stderr)
  -t, --threshold float     Alert threshold (default 0.8)
  -n, --notify              Enable desktop notifications
  -w, --webhook string      Webhook URL for alerts
  -1, --once                Fetch once and exit (no live monitoring)
```

### Examples
```bash
# Interactive monitor
caam monitor

# tmux status bar integration
caam monitor --format brief --once

# Alert mode with desktop notifications
caam monitor --alert --notify --threshold 80

# JSON output for scripting
caam monitor --format json --once | jq '.profiles[] | select(.usage.primary_percent > 80)'

# Monitor specific provider
caam monitor --provider claude
```

## Integration Points

### TUI Integration
Add monitor panel to existing TUI (internal/tui/):
- Real-time usage bars for each profile
- Alert indicators
- Quick switch actions

### Daemon Integration  
Monitor can run as part of daemon for:
- Background alert generation
- Desktop notifications
- Webhook triggers

## Deliverables
1. Monitor struct and state management
2. Background polling loop
3. Table renderer (rich ASCII)
4. Brief renderer (one-line)
5. JSON renderer
6. Alert system with thresholds
7. Desktop notification integration
8. Webhook notification
9. caam monitor command
10. TUI integration (optional)

## Dependencies
- internal/usage/ (for fetching rate limits)
- internal/health/ (for health status)
- internal/db/ (for cooldown status)
- EPIC: Background Auth Pool Daemon (for pool status)

## Enables
- Real-time visibility into account status
- Proactive notifications before limits hit
- tmux/screen integration for always-visible status
- Scripting and automation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:16:21.518070780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:48.238883113Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:48.238883113Z","close_reason":"Implemented: Monitor struct, background polling, table/brief/json/alert renderers, desktop/webhook notifications (internal/notify/), caam monitor command with all formats. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-fu2w","depends_on_id":"caam-3qdv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:20:48.810497527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-fwl","title":"SQLite database setup and migrations","description":"## Purpose
Set up SQLite database infrastructure for usage analytics and activity tracking.

## Background
SQLite was chosen over BoltDB (used by codex-pool) for its query flexibility.
We need a migration system to handle schema evolution as features are added.

## Technical Requirements

### Database Location
```
~/.caam/data/caam.db
```

### Migration System
```go
// internal/db/migrations.go
type Migration struct {
    Version int
    Name    string
    Up      string
    Down    string
}

var migrations = []Migration{
    {
        Version: 1,
        Name:    "initial_schema",
        Up: `
            CREATE TABLE schema_version (
                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                applied_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            );
            
            CREATE TABLE activity_log (
                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
                provider TEXT NOT NULL,
                profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                details TEXT,
                duration_seconds INTEGER
            );
            
            CREATE TABLE profile_stats (
                provider TEXT NOT NULL,
                profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                total_activations INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                total_active_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                last_activated DATETIME,
                last_error DATETIME,
                PRIMARY KEY (provider, profile_name)
            );
            
            CREATE INDEX idx_activity_timestamp ON activity_log(timestamp);
            CREATE INDEX idx_activity_provider ON activity_log(provider, profile_name);
        `,
    },
}

func RunMigrations(db *sql.DB) error {
    // Apply pending migrations
}
```

### Database Manager
```go
// internal/db/db.go
type DB struct {
    conn *sql.DB
}

func Open() (*DB, error) {
    // Ensure directory exists
    // Open connection with WAL mode
    // Run migrations
}

func (d *DB) Close() error
```

## Dependencies
- Uses modernc.org/sqlite (CGO-free driver)
- Directory utilities from existing caam code

## Files to Create
- internal/db/db.go
- internal/db/migrations.go
- internal/db/db_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Database created automatically on first use
- [ ] Migrations run idempotently
- [ ] WAL mode enabled for concurrent reads
- [ ] Tests verify migration system works
- [ ] Graceful handling of corrupt database","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:22:11.418686244Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T00:57:08.742627263Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T00:57:08.742627263Z","close_reason":"Added SQLite DB infrastructure with migrations, WAL mode, and corruption recovery.","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"caam-fwl","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting SQLite DB + migrations: add internal/db with Open (WAL mode) + migration runner + tests (including corrupt DB handling). Will use modernc.org/sqlite per spec.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:52:57Z"},{"id":18,"issue_id":"caam-fwl","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented internal/db with modernc.org/sqlite: Open/OpenAt creates ~/.caam/data/caam.db (or CAAM_HOME), enables WAL + foreign_keys, runs idempotent migrations, and auto-renames corrupt DB files before recreating. Added migration + Open tests.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:56:57Z"}]}

@@ -33,15 +33,38 @@ type ThemeOptions struct {
 	ReducedMotion bool
 }
 
+// StatusSeverity indicates the severity of a status message.
+type StatusSeverity int
+
+const (
+	StatusInfo StatusSeverity = iota
+	StatusSuccess
+	StatusWarning
+	StatusError
+)
+
+func (s StatusSeverity) String() string {
+	switch s {
+	case StatusSuccess:
+		return "success"
+	case StatusWarning:
+		return "warning"
+	case StatusError:
+		return "error"
+	default:
+		return "info"
+	}
+}
+
 // Theme defines the active palette and rendering characteristics.
 type Theme struct {
 	Mode     ThemeMode
 	Contrast ThemeContrast
 	NoColor  bool
 	// ReducedMotion disables animated UI effects (e.g., spinners).
 	ReducedMotion bool
-	Palette  Palette
-	Border   lipgloss.Border
+	Palette       Palette
+	Border        lipgloss.Border
 }
 
 // Palette holds the color tokens used throughout the TUI.
@@ -143,12 +166,12 @@ func NewTheme(opts ThemeOptions) Theme {
 	}
 
 	return Theme{
-		Mode:     opts.Mode,
-		Contrast: opts.Contrast,
-		NoColor:  opts.NoColor,
+		Mode:          opts.Mode,
+		Contrast:      opts.Contrast,
+		NoColor:       opts.NoColor,
 		ReducedMotion: opts.ReducedMotion,
-		Palette:  palette,
-		Border:   border,

 }
 
@@ -310,9 +333,12 @@ type Styles struct {
 	Active       lipgloss.Style
 
 	// Status bar styles
-	StatusBar  lipgloss.Style
-	StatusKey  lipgloss.Style
-	StatusText lipgloss.Style
+	StatusBar     lipgloss.Style
+	StatusKey     lipgloss.Style
+	StatusText    lipgloss.Style
+	StatusSuccess lipgloss.Style
+	StatusWarning lipgloss.Style
+	StatusError   lipgloss.Style
 
 	// Empty state
 	Empty lipgloss.Style
@@ -403,6 +429,10 @@ func NewStyles(theme Theme) Styles {
 		StatusText: lipgloss.NewStyle().
 			Foreground(p.Muted),
 
+		StatusSuccess: statusSeverityStyle(theme, p.Success),
+		StatusWarning: statusSeverityStyle(theme, p.Warning),
+		StatusError:   statusSeverityStyle(theme, p.Danger),
+
 		Empty: lipgloss.NewStyle().
 			Foreground(p.Muted).
 			Italic(true).
@@ -449,3 +479,25 @@ func NewStyles(theme Theme) Styles {
 			Bold(true),
 	}
 }
+
+// StatusSeverityStyle returns the status style for the given severity.
+func (s Styles) StatusSeverityStyle(severity StatusSeverity) lipgloss.Style {
+	switch severity {
+	case StatusSuccess:
+		return s.StatusSuccess
+	case StatusWarning:
+		return s.StatusWarning
+	case StatusError:
+		return s.StatusError
+	default:
+		return s.StatusText
+	}
+}
+
+func statusSeverityStyle(theme Theme, color lipgloss.TerminalColor) lipgloss.Style {
+	style := lipgloss.NewStyle()
+	if theme.NoColor {
+		return style
+	}
+	return style.Foreground(color)
+}
